LAW AND MOTION 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT TAGGED, INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1 BECAUSE A CLAIM IS STATED AT LEAST AS TO RESCISSION AND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 3 BECAUSE RESCISSION IS ADEQUATELY ALLEGED AND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 5 BECAUSE AT LEAST NEGLIGENCE PER SE IS ADEQUATELY PLEADED. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 4 BECAUSE PLAINTIFF LACKS STANDING AND CANNOT PLEAD RELIANCE. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 6 AND 7. MOVING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 301/PJB

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PLAINTIFF/CROSS DEFENDANT TAGGED, INCORPORATED'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 1 BECAUSE A CLAIM IS STATED AT LEAST AS TO RESCISSION AND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 3 BECAUSE RESCISSION IS ADEQUATELY ALLEGED AND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 5 BECAUSE AT LEAST NEGLIGENCE PER SE IS ADEQUATELY PLEADED. SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSE OF ACTION 4 BECAUSE PLAINTIFF LACKS STANDING AND CANNOT PLEAD RELIANCE. SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 6 AND 7. Moving party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
DEFENDANT DANIEL BALSAM'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED WITH 10 DAYS TO ANSWER. PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS NOT NECESSARILY INTERNALLY INCONSISTENT AND THE CONTRACT AT ISSUE IS NOT UNILATERAL. DEFENDANT'S LENGHTY DECLARATION IS IMPROPER AND THE COURT WILL NOT CONSIDER IT. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

Plaintiff Tagged Inc.'S Motion For Order Sealing Confidential Settlememt Agreement
Off Calendar Aug-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
Plaintiff Tagged Inc.'S Motion For Order Sealing Confidential Settlememt Agreement IS DENIED.
THE AGREEMENT'S CONFIDENTIALITY CLAUSE EXCEPTS ITS USE TO ENFORCE THE AGREEMENT AND NOTHING IN THE AGREEMENT JUSTIFIES THE STANDARDS IN RULE OF COURT 2.550. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

* * * THE ORDER SHOULD CONTAIN WHAT NEEDS TO BE DONE WITH THE DOCUMENTS LODGED UNDER SEAL WITH CRC 3.1312 COMPLIANCE * * *

PLAINTIFF TAGGED, INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION'S Motion For Order Sealing Confidential Settlement Agreement IS CONTINUED TO AUGUST 24, 2010 TO BE HEARD WITH THE OTHER MOTIONS. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

Notice Of Motion And Special Motion To Strike
Off Calendar Jul-23-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. TRACY M. CLEMENTS OF THE KRIEG, KELLER & ROMAN LLP, TEL. NO. 249-8314 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
DANEIL BALSAM, TEL. NO. 869-2873, DEFENDANT APPEARING IN PRO PER.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT DANIEL BALSAM'S Motion And Special Motion To Strike IS DENIED; THE COMPLAINT DOES NOT ARISE FROM PROTECTED CONDUCT GIVEN THAT IT SEEKS INTERPRETATION AND ENFORCEMENT OF A PRIVATE CONTRACT; IN ANY EVENT, PLAINTIFF HAS SUFFICIENTLY SHOWN A PROBABILITY OF SUCCESS BY OFFERING A POTENTIAL INTERPRETATION OF THE CONTRACT THAT WOULD SUPPORT RELIEF. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
